meta.title: TypeScript Config – Complete tsconfig.json Guide (2026)
meta.description: Master TypeScript configuration with tsconfig.json. Learn all compiler options, project references, and best practices for different project types.

content.value: tsconfig.json is the configuration file for TypeScript projects. It defines compiler options, file inclusion/exclusion rules, and project settings. Without it, TypeScript uses default settings.

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "lib": ["ES2022"],
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "sourceMap": true,
    "removeComments": true
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist", "**/*.test.ts"]
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "lib": ["ES2020", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "allowJs": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true
  },
  "include": ["src"],
  "references": [{ "path": "./tsconfig.node.json" }]
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "module": "CommonJS",
    "lib": ["ES2020"],
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "sourceMap": true,
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "strict": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist", "**/*.test.ts", "**/*.spec.ts"]
}

value: **Forgetting to set rootDir** – Causes unexpected output structure
**Using outDir instead of rootDir** – Misunderstood paths
**Not using strict mode** – Missing type safety benefits
**Missing declaration files for libraries** – No .d.ts output
**Always enable strict mode** – Catch more errors
**Set correct target** – Based on your environment

content.value: tsconfig.json is the heart of every TypeScript project. Understanding compiler options enables you to optimize builds, enforce coding standards, and configure TypeScript for any environment. Start with strict mode and customize based on your project needs.

faq.question: What is tsconfig.json?
faq.answer: Configuration file that specifies compiler options and files to include in a TypeScript project.

faq.question: Strict mode vs noImplicitAny?
faq.answer: Strict enables all strict family flags; noImplicitAny only prevents implicit any.

faq.question: What are project references?
faq.answer: Feature that allows TypeScript projects to depend on each other for better build performance.

faq.question: How to see effective config?
faq.answer: Run `tsc --showConfig` to see the resolved configuration.

quiz.question: Which option enables all strict type checking flags?
